bugs.summary: The patch moves method-name length validation after subscribe/unsubscribe dispatch, so oversized *_subscribe method names are classified by subscription parsing first.
bugs.impact: WebSocket JSON-RPC requests with method names longer than maxMethodNameLength return the wrong validation error and bypass the generic method length guard for subscription-shaped names.
acceptableFixes: Check len(msg.Method) > maxMethodNameLength before msg.isSubscribe() and msg.isUnsubscribe() dispatch.
Keep subscription parsing from shadowing the method-name length validation error.

mustAvoidClaims: Using upstream PR titles, issue numbers, commit messages, or git history as evidence.
Relying on private repro.sh, gold-key.draft.json, or reference-fix.patch.
Claiming unrelated broad rewrites outside the proposed patch are required.

difficulty.rationale: Fast deterministic validation-order case; useful as an OSS smoke but likely easier than the concurrency cases.

candidateVisible: sanitized go-ethereum workspace at pinned revision
candidateForbidden: judge prompts, answer keys, run artifacts, and A/B mappings
upstream PR/issue text or commit message

approvedBy: source-only reverse fixture with RED/GREEN repro
approval.notes: Pilot OSS-derived holdout task; provider review pending before promotion to primary release gate.
